selected_important_turn.text: okay friend you can have 3 firewood and 3 water is that good enough?
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first large concession by YOU after the opponent politely rejected the opening offer. It frames the negotiation as conciliatory and sets the tone for the remainder of the interaction (you give away all water and firewood).
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The offer is overly concessional and specific without first eliciting the opponent's priorities or explaining tradeoffs. By giving away nearly everything except food, YOU reduce leverage for obtaining needed compensation (or information) and may invite the opponent to press harder on scarce items (food).
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Before making such a large concession, ask a clarifying question about the opponent's priorities or explain the tradeoff you need (e.g., ask whether food or water matters most, or suggest a trade that preserves some water for you in exchange for conceding more firewood). Make concessions tied to explicit tradeoffs rather than unconditional.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: slightly up — clearer tradeoffs could reveal integrative options and prevent wasteful concessions
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — tying concessions to tradeoffs yields a more balanced split
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — clarifying needs reduces back-and-forth and prevents later re-openings
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase — asking and explaining signals respect and builds trust more than unilateral concessions

value2reason.Low: I already have an excess of firewood and know how to create more.
value2reason.Medium: There is no lake so I have nowhere to get water, to shower, etc
value2reason.High: There is no lake near by so I can't fish.

value2reason.Low: i will need this to quench my thirst
value2reason.Medium: i will need this to make me warm as there will be a cold weather out there
value2reason.High: i will need this so i do not end up starving

dialogue.text: hello friend i will like to sugest if i can have 2 packages of food and 1 firewood,you can have i food 2 firewood and 3 water,let me know what you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I don't think that's going to work for me, but I'm sure we can come up with an agreement.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: okay friend you can have 3 firewood and 3 water is that good enough?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm prioritizing food for survival, but I'm open to trade. How do you value food versus firewood or water?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I value food most, then water, then firewood. I’m willing to trade firewood for water or food. What’s your priority?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I see. Since you value food most, let's focus on balancing food first. I'll give you all 3 water and 3 firewood packages in exchange for 2 food packages. That way, we both get what we need most, and I can still secure some warmth and hydration.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm not sure I can agree to that. Giving up 2 food packages for 3 water and 3 firewood would leave me with only 1 food package, which isn't enough. How about I take 2 food, 1 water, and 2 firewood, and you take the rest? That way we both get some of what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your concern. Let's adjust. I'll take 2 food, 2 firewood, and 1 water, while you take 1 food, 1 firewood, and 2 water. This way, we both get some of our priorities met without giving up too much.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think that split still doesn't align with my priorities. I need more food and water. How about I take 2 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood, and you take 1 food, 1 water, and 2 firewood? It balances our needs better.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I see your point. Let's try a different approach. How about I give you 2 food and 1 firewood in exchange for 2 water? That way, I keep more food for myself while still giving you some of what you need, and we both get a fair share.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think we can agree on 2 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood for me, and 1 food, 1 water, and 2 firewood for you. It balances our needs and priorities fairly.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I agree to this split. You get 1 food, 1 water, and 2 firewood, and I take 2 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood. It balances our needs and priorities fairly.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Deal confirmed: I take 2 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood. You take 1 food, 1 water, and 2 firewood. Agreement reached.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
